name: Ísafjörður
summary: Isafjordur is the biggest town in Iceland's wild Westfjords, tucked on a narrow sandy spit with steep cliffs towering all around. Arctic foxes prowl nearby wilderness, the thunderous Dynjandi waterfall tumbles like a giant bridal veil, and in summer the sun barely sets at all.
funFacts: The Westfjords sit on Iceland's oldest rocks, around 14 to 16 million years old, formed by ancient volcanoes long before the rest of the island existed.
The Arctic fox is Iceland's only native land mammal. It walked across the frozen sea from Greenland thousands of years ago, before any people arrived.
Dynjandi waterfall thunders down 100 metres in a wide, lacy curtain shape. Its name actually means 'thunderous' in Icelandic because of how loudly it roars.
Isafjordur's tiny airport is one of the trickiest in the world. Pilots must do a sharp turn between mountains seconds before landing, almost like a video game level.
Hornstrandir, just north of town, was once full of villages but everyone moved away by 1952. Today Arctic foxes rule the empty valleys and abandoned houses.
thingsToDo: Tour Vigur Island by boat to walk among 100,000 puffins, see Iceland's only windmill, and post a card from Europe's smallest post office.
Visit the Westfjords Heritage Museum at Nedstikaupstadur, exploring 18th-century timber buildings packed with old fishing boats, diving suits, and 190 accordions.
Drive about 1.5 hours to Dynjandi waterfall and climb the rocky path past six smaller waterfalls to stand right beside the giant bridal veil.
Hop through the tunnel to Bolungarvik and meet a guide in lambskin clothing at Osvor Maritime Museum, a replica 1800s fishing camp by the sea.
Kayak inside the calm fjord with a local guide, spotting jellyfish, seabirds, and maybe seals while mountains rise straight up from the water.
Eat a famous fish feast at Tjoruhusid restaurant, where servers bring sizzling pans of freshly caught Westfjords fish straight to your wooden table.

images.alt: The colourful little town of Ísafjörður tucked under tall mountains beside a calm blue fjord.
The huge Dynjandi waterfall tumbling down a mountain like a giant bridal veil.
A fluffy brown Arctic fox standing on Icelandic grass in its summer coat.
The towering Látrabjarg sea cliffs, the biggest bird cliffs in the North Atlantic.
Cute puffins and razorbills perched together on the grassy edge of the Látrabjarg cliffs.
